item.title: SA heavy vehicle freight reforms fast-tracked to allow heavier, longer trucks
item.description: The South Australian government says recent changes to heavy vehicle freight limits will lower fuel costs and reduce the number of freight trucks needed on the road.

item.title: Tintinara farm owner convicted over clearing 112 hectares of bushland
item.description: The owner of a farm in South Australia's south-east is convicted and fined for clearing 112 hectares of bushland, but the penalty is much less than the $2.2 million he would have had to pay for a permit for the work.

item.title: Tom Duffy to start for Brisbane Broncos, Adam Reynolds ruled out of Cowboys clash
item.description: Brisbane captain Adam Reynolds has succumbed to injury and will not play against North Queensland.

item.title: People living in vehicles forced to choose between diesel and food as fuel prices remain high
item.description: The high cost of fuel is making it even more difficult for people living in their cars to afford food, electricity and transport.

item.title: Four people to face court for alleged offences 'connected' to murder of Adelaide father Chris Robertson
item.description: Four people have been arrested by police for alleged offences "connected" to the alleged murder of Adelaide father-of-two Chris Robertson last year.

item.title: $1 million reward for information on unsolved death of Harmony Bryant
item.description: Police hope a $1 million reward will produce fresh information about the death of a 26-year-old Kyogle woman, who was found suffering severe burns in bushland on the NSW Mid North Coast.

item.title: $10m Winx filly Quinceanera won't race, but co-owner has 'no regrets'
item.description: An owner says she has "no regrets" after announcing that the first filly from champion race mare Winx will never race.

item.title: Victorian opposition promises 200 more PSOs to patrol railway stations across Melbourne
item.description: The Victorian opposition is promising to recruit hundreds more protective service officers if it wins government in November.

item.title: Wests Tigers looking to win three NRL matches in a row for the first time since 2018
item.description: The last time Wests Tigers won three matches in a row, Ivan Cleary was still the club's coach and Benji Marshall had just begun his comeback as a player. Now, after eight years of waiting, they have the chance to end that streak.

item.title: Nitrous oxide a 'significant contributing factor' in Canberra teenager's drowning death, coroner finds
item.description: An 18-year-old was heavily impaired by nitrous oxide, known as "nangs", when his car entered a pond, prompting a coroner to call for a review of the gas's availability in the ACT.

item.title: Jury hears Maree Crabtree, on trial for murder of son Jonathan, told police he would inject 'whatever he can get his hands on'
item.description: Maree Mavis Crabtree is on trial in Brisbane charged with the murder of Jonathan Crabtree in July 2017, and his earlier attempted murder.

item.title: Fremantle convenience store crackdown to curb 'under the counter' illegal nicotine sales
item.description: The City of Fremantle voted unanimously at Wednesday's council meeting to amend local planning laws, in a bid to stop more convenience stores that sell illicit tobacco products from opening.

item.title: Gang rapist on the run after escaping custody at Sydney hospital
item.description: A man convicted of gang raping a 14-year-old girl in a Western Sydney park has escaped from custody while at a hospital. Follow live.

item.title: Jury retires to consider verdict in Irma Palasics murder trial after weeks of evidence
item.description: Justice David Mossop told the jury it had a "complex" and "substantial" task after the court was presented with 53 witnesses, 14 statements or recordings, and 97 exhibits.

item.title: Iran war fuel crisis financially benefiting Russia and Vladimir Putin
item.description: Donald Trump used to love using the word "winning", but out of the wreckage of the Iran war and the world's oil supplies slowing, there is one man truly winning: Russia's Vladimir Putin.

item.title: Police calling for witnesses after wanted man left with life-threatening injuries
item.description: Police allege the 34-year-old "was seen to be acting suspiciously" near homes at Bray Park near Murwillumbah when he was confronted.

item.title: Sydney basketball court shortage in spotlight after Kings' NBL victory
item.description: Basketball NSW says there is a 10,000-person-long waitlist to play, but hundreds of new courts will be needed to meet demand.

item.title: Crows get home against Blues in the wet in Gather Round opener
item.description: The Adelaide Crows send the fans home happy after ending its losing streak by beating Carlton at a packed Adelaide Oval to start Gather Round. Follow live.

item.title: Emperor penguins, Antarctic fur seals now endangered due to climate change
item.description: The iconic emperor penguin and the Antarctic fur seal are edging closer to extinction, according to a projection by the world's largest environmental network.

item.title: Bulldogs stun misfiring Panthers in big boilover victory
item.description: The Penrith Panthers unbeaten start to the NRL season has been ended in comprehensive fashion by the Bulldogs at Stadium Australia.

item.title: Alcoa rejects concerns over increased mercury levels at its Wagerup refinery
item.description: The US mining giant says increased mercury emissions from its Wagerup refinery are a by-product of higher production, amid a Conservation Council appeal against its operating licence.

item.title: Police investigation underway after officer pushes Indigenous woman to the ground during arrest in Broome
item.description: An internal police investigation is underway, after video footage emerged online showing a police officer pushing a woman to the ground in Western Australia's far north.

item.title: Man charged with murder over Sunshine Coast woman's 'suspicious' kangaroo crash
item.description: NT police have charged a 33-year-old man with murder over the "suspicious death" of his female partner, who was found dead on an outback road over the weekend, after he alleged she was injured in a crash involving a kangaroo.

item.title: Combatting concussion in contact sport
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Israel and Hezbollah trade fire, as US and Iran prepare for peace talks — as it happened
item.description: Israel says it has planned direct talks with Lebanon, as it continues to trade fire amid the Iran ceasefire.

item.title: Weekly news quiz: A historical orbit, emotional support animals and a tiny outback town
item.description: It's been a big week in the news. Did you catch these stories?

item.title: Do you ever need to throw out your old bath towels?
item.description: Expert tips on getting the most out of your favourite bath towels and when it might be time to replace them.

item.title: Inside the church of Pete Hegseth, the man who has been leading the US through war
item.description: The ABC visits US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth's church, whose leaders challenge the legitimacy of democracy and key principles in the US constitution.

item.title: How the PM's Singapore visit could help Australia's fuel supply
item.description: Experts say visits like Anthony Albanese's trip to Singapore are important for securing Australia's energy supplies. As one observer puts it: "When you don't have the stock, you need friends."

item.title: Illegal and legal wildlife trade fuelling animal-to-human disease transmission, report finds
item.description: Of the 2,079 mammal species traded worldwide, 41 per cent share at least one pathogen with humans, according to new data released today.

item.title: The NDIS's 'social licence' isn't just at risk of eroding – it's close to collapse
item.description: The NDIS's reputation isn't just at risk of coming under pressure, it's well and truly crumbling, write Nas Campanella and Evan Young.

item.title: Taylor and Canavan are chalk and cheese – and that's a problem for Taylor
item.description: If you want a basic measure of the difference between Liberal leader Angus Taylor and Nationals leader Matt Canavan, compare their reactions to US President Donald Trump's extraordinary threat this week that "a whole civilisation will die tonight".

item.title: Goalkeeper Andrew Redmayne marks 300th A-League game on home turf
item.description: His career began as a substitute for Mark Bosnich almost 20 years ago, now Andrew Redmayne is joining the A-League's exclusive 300 club.

item.title: Jog for Joe aims to raise awareness of male eating disorders in Australia
item.description: Joe Cappello was 25 years old when he died after a 12-year struggle, and the statistics show many more men are at risk.

item.title: Teenage boy charged over Ipswich crash that killed passenger
item.description: A teenage driver has been charged with dangerous driving after a crash that killed his 16-year-old passenger.

item.title: Sexual misconduct findings permanently added to health practitioners' records
item.description: More than 100 past and current practitioners have had findings of sexual misconduct added to their public-facing records under new reforms to promote transparency.

item.title: Adelaide GP Mark Rogers disqualified for professional misconduct amid 'blatant disregard' for previous orders
item.description: An Adelaide doctor with an "unhealthy obsession" for low-level laser therapy has had his registration cancelled after a tribunal found he demonstrated a "blatant disregard" of previous orders imposed on his registration.

item.title: Three Tasmanian MPs share their experiences with mental health and politics
item.description: A national study has found 70 per cent of politicians feel their job is affecting their mental health. The ABC spoke to three Tasmanian MPs, including the state's premier, about their experiences.

item.title: Safety review links herb in popular cold and flu supplements with life-threatening anaphylaxis
item.description: The TGA says people taking popular cold and flu supplements face the risk of life-threatening anaphylaxis from a herbal ingredient.

item.title: Melania Trump criticises Jeffrey Epstein 'lies' and calls for public hearings
item.description: In an unusual White House address, Melania Trump denies having a friendship with Jeffrey Epstein and complains about "mean-spirited" attempts to smear her.

item.title: NSW drug diversion scheme disproportionately sends Aboriginal people to court, report says
item.description: A BOSCAR report finds NSW Police disproportionately used "discretion" to reject eligible low-level drug offenders from a diversion program — especially Aboriginal people.

item.title: Tiwi Island birder hopes rare photos of endemic owl encourage conservation
item.description: A traditional owner on a Northern Australian island has captured rare photos of an "enchanting" masked owl, adding to the limited knowledge base surrounding the endemic and culturally significant bird.

item.title: How the Parkes Dish is helping track Artemis II on its historic journey
item.description: The Parkes Observatory — also known as The Dish — continues a legacy of supporting lunar missions that started more than 50 years ago, helping Artemis II go where humanity has not since 1972.

item.title: Australian role in Hormuz depends on permanent ceasefire, Marles says — as it happened
item.description: Australia has been involved in talks about assisting the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz but any action can only take place if a ceasefire becomes permanent, Richard Marles says.

item.title: Study finds Tasmanian native forest logging increases potential for more severe bushfires
item.description: Scientists have studied satellite images of a Tasmanian bushfire and found regrowth from extensive logging and recent bushfires has "absolutely" increased the risk of more severe bushfires.

item.title: Southern Queensland farmers take pre-emptive action as dry spell stretches on
item.description: Queensland farmers gripped by a dry spell are turning to drastic and inventive measures: offloading most of their livestock and laying kilometres of water pipelines.

item.title: Low-sensory voting to be introduced statewide at 2026 Victorian election
item.description: State election voting will be made quieter and calmer for neurodivergent voters in Victoria.

item.title: Mark Gregory's mum just wants to know what happened to her son 35 years ago
item.description: Mark Gregory was just 28 when he was killed by a hit-and-run driver while walking home from a party in 1991. His family just wants answers.

item.title: Search for missing Murwillumbah woman Yvonne Whear continues in northern NSW
item.description: The daughter of Yvonne Whear, 73, who went missing in February, says there is no evidence to suggest her mum left her northern NSW home of her own accord.

item.title: Truck driver charged over Sunshine Coast crash that killed three-year-old girl
item.description: A truck driver has been charged with drug driving over a crash that killed a three-year-old girl and critically injured her one-year-old sister.

item.title: Cheat Sheet: Charting Euphoria's long and messy road to season three
item.description: Sam Levinson's controversial teen series has had to overcome four years of actor deaths, character departures and on-set tension to make it to season three.

item.title: Adventure seekers urged to heed caution after three deaths, many rescues at Sunshine Coast's Glass House Mountains
item.description: Authorities and experts are urging visitors to Queensland’s popular Glass House Mountains to heed safety warnings after three deaths in as many weeks.

item.title: Inside the secret Pakistan talks that brokered the fragile Iran war ceasefire
item.description: For a few volatile hours, the war in the Middle East appeared to be tipping past the point of no return — until a last-minute push of diplomacy pulled it back.

item.title: Sean 'Diddy' Combs's four-year prison sentence questioned in appeals court
item.description: Sean "Diddy" Combs's lawyers argue his four-year prison term for prostitution-related charges is too harsh.

item.title: Man charged after $1.1 million of diesel fuel allegedly stolen from unmanned Queensland depots over 12 months
item.description: Police will allege a 48-year-old man used stolen fuel cards to obtain the diesel from unmanned depots over a 12-month period.

item.title: Hills Shire Mayor leads calls to simplify governance of Hawkesbury River
item.description: The Hawkesbury-Nepean River flows through eight NSW council areas and is looked after by 13 agencies, which one mayor says leads to confusion about who is in charge of what.

item.title: Chimpanzee's in Uganda's Kibale National Park fight
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Chimpanzees made "territorial patrols'
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Chimpanzees in Uganda's Kibale National Park wage lethal 'civil war'
item.description: A decades-long study reveals hundreds of chimpanzees waging a violent and deadly conflict against their former friends in their remote forest home.

item.title: High Court urged to throw out reduced sentence for teen killer of Emma Lovell
item.description: Lawyers for the Queensland government have travelled to Canberra to urge the High Court to toss out a reduced sentence for a teen jailed for killing Emma Lovell in 2022.

item.title: Penrith Panthers player Casey McLean faces suspension for high tackle in loss to Canterbury
item.description: Penrith centre Casey McLean is facing a one-week suspension for a high shot that left Canterbury prop Max King with a broken jaw.

item.title: Second midwife charged with manslaughter after failed Newcastle homebirth
item.description: Jordan Michaela Holland is the second midwife charged with manslaughter over the death of a baby after a failed homebirth in Newcastle.

item.title: Rainy weather easing for AFL Gather Round weekend matches, as Barossa welcomes fans
item.description: The Bureau of Meteorology says wet weather conditions will improve for AFL Gather Round matches as the weekend progresses, as the premier shares his ongoing bid to keep the event in South Australia.

item.title: Family of NSW surfer Guy Haymes deliver victim impact statements
item.description: Guy Haymes was left in a vegetative state after an assault during a dispute over $50 in February, 2024.

item.title: Why the Iran war fuel crisis will get worse before it gets better
item.description: The official pledges of the last few days may have placated markets at first, but they have become little more than bargaining chips ahead of this weekend's negotiations between the US and Iran.

item.title: Robert Noel Rogers jailed for life over 2021 murder of Raymond Scopelitis on Sunshine Coast
item.description: Following a trial held in Brisbane, a jury deliberated for almost two days before returning its verdict.

item.title: Seal sleeping on road causes Mornington Peninsula traffic delays
item.description: Locals say they are not surprised the seal, described as a regular visitor, decided to stop for a snooze on the roadway.

item.title: Australia vs Great Britain Billie Jean King Cup: Talia Gibson and Kimberly Birrell lose singles
item.description: Australia's quest to win a place in the Billie Jean King Cup Finals is off to the worst possible start after losses in both opening-day singles matches.

item.title: What to watch and listen to this weekend
item.description: An old favourite, new Courtney Barnett and a tasty Korean vegetarian stew.

item.title: Artemis II crew about to make splash landing on Earth after journey around Moon
item.description: The Orion space capsule will enter the Earth's atmosphere as a burning fireball, travelling at about 40,000 km/h, splashing down off the coast of southern California. Here's a breakdown of how the spacecraft copes with high temperatures, slows down, and what happens as it splashes down.

item.title: Bribbaree grandmother Nerol Doble's family says there will be 'no forgiveness' for son who set her on fire
item.description: NSW grandmother Nerol Doble was murdered by her son Shannon James Doble in 2024 after an argument over the use of a jerry can. During a sentencing hearing, family members told the court of the hell his actions have left them in.

item.title: Afrika Bambaataa, rap icon accused of sexual abuse, dead at 67
item.description: The Universal Zulu Nation founder was a beloved hip hop icon before sexual abuse and trafficking allegations surfaced in the 2010s.

item.title: At the site of an Israeli strike in Beirut, locals say 'there's no Hezbollah here'
item.description: In a residential area in Beirut an entire building had been razed to the ground and a nut shop was destroyed. The locals told the ABC "there's no Hezbollah here".

item.title: Blayze James Noble charged with murder of Christine Joyce Diane Marshall Hunter
item.description: A Sunshine Coast man has been charged with murdering his girlfriend in the Northern Territory. Ahead of his appearance in court, her sister has spoken of her family's heartbreak.

item.title: Man pleads guilty to dealing with millions in cash from illegal tobacco sales
item.description: Malek Alhmedai's hire car had $4 million stashed inside, with the money linked to illegal tobacco sales.

item.title: Commuters trapped in South Eastern Freeway gridlock blast lack of signage
item.description: Commuters stuck queuing for up to four hours following a truck crash on the South Eastern Freeway on Thursday blast the transport department over a lack of signs warning them of the road closure ahead.

item.title: Jury hears Maree Crabtree, on trial for murdering son Jonathan, allegedly claimed she 'wanted to put something in his drink'
item.description: A Gold Coast woman accused of killing her son allegedly called him a "potato" with "no life" and claimed she "wanted to put something in his drink so he will just not be here", a jury heard.

item.title: Man jailed for stomping on head of off-duty police officer in Newtown attack
item.description: Koby Weaver is jailed over his role in what is described as a "brutal" attack which left an off-duty police officer with serious injuries.

item.title: Ian Brooke to remain in prison for killing foster brother in Broome
item.description: Ian Brooke was found not guilty of murder due to his mental impairment, but will have to stay in jail because of a lack of suitable mental health care facilities to house him.

item.title: Dunsborough Primary School left scrambling to pay $130k water bill
item.description: Dunsborough Primary School is racking up massive water bills in comparison to nearby schools, to the detriment of student support and repairs to facilities.

item.title: Dockers launch late fightback to pip Magpies in frantic finish
item.description: Fremantle wins its fourth match in a row after surviving a tense final few minutes against Collingwood at Adelaide Oval.

item.title: Australian War Memorial display on Ben Roberts-Smith altered as refurbished sections unveiled
item.description: As the Australian War Memorial unveils some of its newest elements as part of a $500 million redevelopment, director Matt Anderson confirms it has altered a display dedicated to Ben Roberts-Smith in the wake of his arrest for alleged war crimes.

item.title: WA Police say bones found on Rottnest Island are historic Indigenous remains
item.description: Several human bones that were found on Rottnest Island this week are historic Indigenous remains, WA Police say.

item.title: NT Police investigating death of child found in Palmerston driveway
item.description: Northern Territory Police are investigating the death of a 3-month-old who was found inside a vehicle parked in the driveway of a Palmerston home on Friday.

item.title: St Kilda forward Lance Collard guilty over using homophobic slur
item.description: St Kilda forward Lance Collard has been found guilty of using a homophobic slur against an opponent, leaving his AFL career hanging in the balance.

item.title: ABC 50/50 quiz: This week we're testing your knowledge of a classic Aussie poem, oyster recipes and Itchy and Scratchy
item.description: Every question has only two options: one is right, and the other is wrong. You have an even chance of guessing correctly each time.

item.title: Debra Adelaide reflects on friendship with Gabrielle Carey in When I Am Sixty-Four
item.description: Debra Adelaide became friends with Gabrielle Carey when they were 12. After Carey died by suicide five decades later, Adelaide tried to address her unanswered questions in a novel.

item.title: How Australia is navigating an oil market in crisis
item.description: Australia is stitching together a steady supply of fuel through a patchwork of relationships. But how long can that last?

item.title: Iran uses sea mine threat to 'maintain chokehold' over Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Iran claims to have littered a central shipping passage in the Strait of Hormuz with sea mines, in a move analysts say could be a "coercive threat" to maintain its grip on the crucial waterway.

item.title: Trump threatens Iran with warships ahead of 'make-or-break' talks — as it happened
item.description: US Vice-President JD Vance is due to arrive in Pakistan as both sides level accusations of ceasefire breaches.

item.title: Israel's ambassador concedes Iran's chokehold on Strait of Hormuz was not expected before war
item.description: Israeli ambassador Hillel Newman admits his country was surprised by the scope of Iran's response to the Middle East war and that it did not expect Gulf states to be targeted in retaliation strikes.

item.title: Jess Hull and Claudia Hollingsworth speak after the dramatic women's 1,500m final
item.description: Sport news, live scores, features, analysis and photos from the world of sport. Comprehensive coverage of AFL, cricket, football, rugby league and rugby union. Audio interviews. News stories and highlights in video. Sports news bulletin.

item.title: Milk prices may need to increase as dairy farmers hit hard by fuel and fertiliser costs
item.description: Dairy farmers across Australia are battling crippling fuel and fertiliser costs. The industry is now calling for a 30-cent-per-litre rise in milk prices on supermarket shelves.

item.title: What to do if you get a fishhook injury and how to avoid one
item.description: What do you do when the hook catches you instead of the fish? Charter fishers and a medical expert weigh in.

item.title: Renewed push for completion of dumped Victorian Murray Basin freight rail project
item.description: North-west Victorian farmers are pushing for the completion of the Murray Basin Rail Project, more than seven years after it was originally slated to be finished.

item.title: Long-range goals and their impact on the 2026 AFL season
item.description: In a league where inside-50s are so often king, teams with prowess from outside the arc are making a major impact to start the 2026 AFL season.

item.title: Astronauts hoisted to recovery ship after 'perfect bullseye' landing — as it happened
item.description: The crew on board the Orion has returned to Earth, splashing down in the Pacific Ocean in a perfect '"bullseye". Read back over the day's event in our Artemis II blog.

item.title: Police appeal for information about mysterious shooting death of Richard Wills at Ouyen
item.description: Richard Wills was found shot and buried in a shallow grave at his rural property in Victoria's north-west earlier this week.

item.title: British man who avoided deportation after child sex offence could face fresh visa review
item.description: A British child sex offender who avoided deportation is facing a possible visa review, after an ABC investigation.

item.title: Armidale museum freezes dozens of koalas for taxidermy and research
item.description: Most people usually drive past animals killed on the road, but in Armidale, they stop, bag them up, and drop them off at the local museum.

item.title: Rotting sperm whale carcass forces relocation of Tasmania's largest open water swim
item.description: A 40-tonne sperm whale carcass has forced the relocation of Tasmania's biggest open water swim. Organisers of the event had hoped it would be moved, but the state's environment department says that was not feasible.

item.title: Tamworth teen Max Lye is no chicken in the egg business
item.description: While many teenagers spend their spare time online gaming or playing sport, 14-year-old Max Lye hangs out with a bunch of chooks and dreams of owning his own egg farm.

item.title: Plans to move residents off Cocos (Keeling) Islands walked back after backlash
item.description: After seething criticism from residents, the Commonwealth has released its final plan to protect the Cocos (Keeling) Islands from rising sea levels.

item.title: Hobart City Mission repairing donated clothing to reduce textile waste
item.description: Every year, lots of new textile items are imported to the Australian market … about 1.55 billion of them. Fast fashion is a leading contributor to overproduction and makes reuse difficult.

item.title: Cyclone Maila weakening and slowing as it heads towards Australia
item.description: After causing damage to islands in the Pacific, Cyclone Maila is downgraded as it heads towards Australia.

item.title: Fuel crisis sees less motorists on Sydney roads but small rise in public transport use, data shows
item.description: It is exactly six weeks since the conflict began in the MIddle East and data suggests Sydney motorists have changed how they travel in order to cope with record fuel prices.

item.title: By rejecting his bid for appeal the High Court has made Bruce Lehrmann's defamation case a complete failure
item.description: The High Court was Bruce Lehrmann's last legal avenue to challenge a Federal Court ruling that, on the balance of probabilities, he raped Brittany Higgins — now that avenue has closed.

item.title: Cyclone Vaianu forces Supercars to cancel third and final Taupō race
item.description: A looming cyclone forces organisers to cancel the Supercars race in Taupō on New Zealand's north island.

item.title: This rich, meat-free curry is a budget-friendly way to make a massaman - ABC News
item.description: Mushrooms are a cost-effective way to make a hearty massaman that is also vego and vegan-friendly.

item.title: Highland cattle breeder numbers climb in WA as interest spreads and agritourism grows
item.description: Registered Highland cattle breeders have more than tripled in Western Australia in the past five years. The shaggy Scottish breed is gaining popularity across WA, with more breeders interested in taking on the cattle and strong demand from agritourism.

item.title: Thailand's fishing industry in 'big trouble' after Middle East war doubles fuel prices
item.description: Tens of thousands of small and commercial fishing vessels in Thailand are stranded on shore, no longer able to afford diesel, which has more than doubled in price since the Iran war began.

item.title: NT police no longer calling for information on death of man found near road in Tennant Creek
item.description: Northern Territory police say they are no longer calling for information about a man who was found dead near a roadway in the remote town of Tennant Creek.

item.title: 'May as well have gone to the pub': Bellamy questions Storm after fourth loss
item.description: The Warriors have ended their decade-long run of outs against Melbourne in style as the Storm officially hit rock bottom following a heavy loss.

item.title: Israel's mass killing has forced Donald Trump to think about Lebanon
item.description: For weeks, Israel has been intensifying its deadly strikes across Lebanon, and Donald Trump has had little to say. But now he has been forced to make it a priority.

item.title: Four dead after fatal crashes on WA roads
item.description: Four people have died on WA roads after two separate crashes in the space of just over 15 hours.

item.title: Vance says no deal struck with Iran after 21 hours of negotiations — as it happened
item.description: US Vice-President JD Vance says 21 hours of negotiations with Iranian delegates in Pakistan have failed to produce a deal — as it happened.

item.title: Adelaide pubs busy as AFL Gather Round brings in punters, but future of event remains uncertain
item.description: Occupancy rates and food and drink sales are up on last year's Gather Round, says the Australian Hotels Association, despite concerns fuel prices would deter interstate visitors.

item.title: Hard Quiz: Still trying to prove you're good at trivia? You're not fooling me
item.description: I applaud your courage for having a go at this quiz. That's about it, though.

item.title: Disabled artists challenge stereotypes in Acts of Inheritance at Bathurst Regional Art Gallery
item.description: Paper dolls, wrestlers and cloudscapes appear in an exhibition by a Western Sydney artist collective at Bathurst Regional Art Gallery.

item.title: Katy Steele on revisiting Little Birdy, young fame, and stripping songs to their core
item.description: The Little Birdy frontwoman is getting in touch with her old self on her new album and tour.

item.title: The Iran war prompted a cut to the fuel excise, so why is diesel so expensive?
item.description: As the war in the Middle East rages on, diesel prices are continuing to rise despite efforts to stabilise costs. So why is this happening, can anything be done about it and is the worst yet to come?

item.title: The children feared to be at highest risk of missing out under Thriving Kids
item.description: With just months to go until Thriving Kids starts, parents and experts are warning a particular cohort of children is at risk of falling through the cracks.

item.title: The last-resort decision to eject from a fighter jet at supersonic speed
item.description: A retired F-15 pilot who survived one of the fastest known fighter jet ejections in history, describes what two US airmen would have faced when they did the same in Iran a week ago.

item.title: US defence department's New Mexico test track
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: How Jono Ridler's 90-day swim could inspire New Zealand to protect its oceans
item.description: Jono Ridler swam 1,367 kilometres down New Zealand's east coast to raise awareness for ocean health and to end bottom trawling. His exploits have galvanised a nation.

item.title: Regional Australia leads GP bulk-billing surge as costs to see doctors continue to rise
item.description: A new report shows regional and remote towns are rapidly lifting their GP bulk-billing rates while out-of-pocket costs rise across the country.

item.title: Human cannonball continues family tradition at Sydney Royal Easter Show
item.description: David Smith grew up watching his father being shot out of a cannon, so when the time came, he said it was inevitable he would follow in his dad's flight path.

item.title: Life as a human cannonball.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Nick Xenophon-founded SA Best party comes to an end with MLC Connie Bonaros losing seat
item.description: SA Best, the party formed by stunt-loving politician Nick Xenophon, has closed its chapter in South Australian political history with its final member Connie Bonaros losing her seat in last month's state election.

item.title: Tropical Cyclone Maila falls to tropical low ahead of Qld landfall this week
item.description: A tropical cyclone bound for the Queensland coastline has weakened to a tropical low, ahead of its arrival this week.

item.title: Cameron Young, Jason Day apply pressure to Rory McIlroy in third round of Masters
item.description: It will not be a procession to a second straight Masters title for Rory McIlroy, after a tense third round at Augusta National.

item.title: Liberals to offer $5,000 to overseas officers to relocate under plan to recruit 3,000 police
item.description: The opposition says officers from the UK, Ireland and New Zealand would be offered $5,000 to relocate under a plan to recruit 3,000 new officers.

item.title: NASA's Artemis II crew speaks for first time after historic mission
item.description: Artemis crew members have described their mission as "the greatest dream on Earth" after landing on Saturday and setting a new deep-space travel record.

item.title: Artemis II crew speak publicly for first time since splashdown
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Transport Minister Catherine King casts doubt on road user tax over EV uptake concerns
item.description: A new tax on road users, including electric vehicles, has been cast into doubt by the transport minister, who says now may not be the time for it.

item.title: US-Iran Conflict and Ongoing Fuel Crisis
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: NASAs Artemis II Moon Landing in pictures with Mike Bowers and Fiona Katsaukas
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Floods, damage to New Zealand's North Island as Cyclone Vaianu makes landfall
item.description: Floods and power outages are reported in New Zealand, as authorities warn the worst damage from Cyclone Vaianu is yet to come.

item.title: Indigenous leaders call for greater protections for Rottnest Island after discovery of Indigenous remains
item.description: Aboriginal leaders call for greater protections for Rottnest Island after the discovery of Indigenous remains during construction work at the popular holiday resort last week.

item.title: NT coroner hands down attempted murder-suicide findings, detailing signs of coercive control
item.description: The Northern Territory coroner has found an attempted murder-suicide in Darwin was a "tragic outcome" of coercive control.

item.title: Love story: When love means donating a kidney to your brother
item.description: Despite a 10-year age gap and geographical separation, Sajarn and Codey Stow have retained a close bond since childhood. That bond has only strengthened since Codey received a donor kidney from his brother last November.

item.title: Indonesia's first fully AI-animated show Legenda Bertuah gets mixed reaction
item.description: A television show that uses generative artificial intelligence to animate Indonesian folktales is creating a stir.

item.title: Police locate hiker who sheltered overnight after missing for 24 hours in Victoria's north-west
item.description: Police have located a hiker who separated from his group and was missing for more than 24 hours in Victoria's north-west.

item.title: Eels booed by their fans after 42-point loss, Tigers carve up Knights
item.description: Parramatta coach Jason Ryles says Eels fans had a right to boo their team after watching the Gold Coast Titans score nine tries in a 52-10 belting at Western Sydney Stadium.

item.title: Motorcyclist killed, others injured in Scenic Rim traffic crash
item.description: Emergency services were called to Tamborine Mountain Road in the Scenic Rim after reports two cars and a motorcycle had collided.

item.title: WA diesel stockpile being considered by government as nation grapples with fuel worries
item.description: The WA government is considering creating its own "strategic stockpile" of diesel, saying the state's size and the reliance of the agriculture and resources industries on the fuel warrants a separate reserve.

item.title: Labour hire firm iComply's collapse leaves PALM scheme workers millions out of pocket
item.description: Prominent labour hire company iComply owed more than $12 million when it collapsed, according to a damning liquidator's report, with workers chasing millions in superannuation.

item.title: Is sitting with your legs crossed actually bad for you?
item.description: The healthiest sitting position is often the one you do not hold for the next hour.

item.title: Police officer recovers from facial injuries as man who allegedly attacked him granted bail
item.description: A 23-year-old man who allegedly assaulted a police officer in the New South Wales Hunter Valley has been granted bail, drawing the ire of the police community.

item.title: Key takeaways from the Islamabad peace talks between US and Iran
item.description: After an absolute marathon of overnight negotiations and historic high-level talks between the United States and Iran, a deal to end the war that has thrown the Middle East and global energy supplies into chaos has not been reached.

item.title: Queen Mary of Denmark's father dies in Tasmania aged 84
item.description: The Australian-born Queen of Denmark last visited her father Professor John Donaldson during a royal visit to Tasmania last month.

item.title: Will AI bring a jobs crisis or a future of abundance?
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Rory McIlroy joins exclusive group with second straight Masters victory
item.description: Rory McIlroy becomes just the fourth man in history to win back-to-back Masters titles after holding off a star-studded field in the final round at Augusta.

item.title: This week in history quiz, April 13: Jump in, we're going back in time
item.description: Which state had Australia's only communist MP? And where did the Titanic sink?

item.title: Behind solar power's meteoric rise is a little-known Australian
item.description: Solar power is forecast to overtake coal as the world's biggest source of electricity next year, and its growth shows no signs of slowing down. Behind its success is a little-known Australian inventor.

item.title: The 45 phone pings that traced the last known steps of Celine Cremer
item.description: Belgian backpacker Celine Cremer was on the trip of a lifetime, but on a hike in Tasmania she left the trail and was never seen again. It took a private investigator, a YouTuber, loved ones and an army of volunteers to find answers.

item.title: Kim Jong Un and daughter observe military drills
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Iranians feel anger, mistrust and relief after the ceasefire
item.description: While the bombs may have stopped falling on Iran for now, Iranians who've been speaking with ABC News say the mood on the ground is anything but happy.

item.title: Live: Strait blockade looms as Trump says he 'doesn't care' about further talks
item.description: US Central Command say it will begin implementing a blockade of "all maritime traffic entering and exiting Iranian ports" from midnight tonight AEST. Follow live.

item.title: Tasmanian tourist attraction West Coast Wilderness Railway facing uncertain future
item.description: The West Coast Wilderness Railway in Tasmania is a beloved attraction and an important employer. The state government says it wants the railway to have a sustainable future, but hasn't said how that could be achieved.

item.title: Victorian government's Big Housing Build faced budget blowout in early days of landmark program
item.description: Internal documents the Victorian government tried to withhold reveal that a landmark social housing construction project was facing a significant budget blowout two years after it was unveiled.

item.title: Viktor Orbán concedes defeat in Hungary election
item.description: After 16 years in power, the powerful far-right leader says he will serve in opposition, with centre-right ⁠candidate Péter Magyar's Tisza Party ​on course for a super majority.

item.title: Supreme Court hears dispute over possession of impressionist Frederick McCubbin's former home
item.description: A court has heard claims the former home of an Australian impressionist is not safe to walk through, with "black mould, evidence of vermin and wheelie bins collecting rainwater".

item.title: Queensland's north set for soaking from ex-Cyclone Maila as dangerous surf hits the south
item.description: Beautiful one day, heavy rain, dangerous surf and frosts, the next — Queensland faces a mixed bag of weather throughout the state over the coming days.

item.title: Youth mental health centre to close amid Kiama Council budget crisis
item.description: A NSW South Coast community says the proposed closure of a mental health and welfare centre could result in more youth suicides.

item.title: ACT family violence service warns $292m federal funding boost may not reach frontline
item.description: More than $290 million in federal funding is on the table for domestic violence services nationwide, but Canberra providers are concerned about how it will be distributed and tracked by the ACT government.

item.title: Major setback for beekeepers as varroa mite chemical resistance detected again
item.description: There are growing fears that a second varroa mite strain could enter Australia, as beekeepers lose key chemical options for controlling the tiny parasites of honey bees.

item.title: Australia not asked to help Hormuz blockade as PM names new defence chief — as it happened
item.description: Australia has not received a request to participate in US President Donald Trump's blockade of the Strait of Hormuz, Anthony Albanese says.

item.title: ASX slips as oil surges back above $US100 a barrel on US threat of Gulf blockade — as it happened
item.description: Failed US-Iran peace talks send oil prices surging higher and the Australian share market lower.

item.title: Cyclone Maila kills at least 11 people in Papua New Guinea
item.description: The Australian government will give $2.5 million to support relief and recovery efforts in Papua New Guinea and Solomon Islands after Tropical Cyclone Maila.

item.title: Inside Ukraine’s secret network of 3D printing farms building bombs
item.description: In spare rooms and backyard sheds, volunteers across Europe are 3D printing drone bomb parts to supply Ukraine's military.

item.title: Rediscovered sheet music performed at 150th anniversary of Point Pass Immanuel Lutheran Church
item.description: A South Australian church celebrates 150 years with a special performance of sacred music transcribed by early-20th-century parishioners.

item.title: Coral Coast tourism hit with mass cancellations after Cyclone Narelle
item.description: A whale shark boat skipper says one-off payments for tour operators will make a "small difference", as ex-Tropical Cyclone Narelle's aftermath drags on.

item.title: Wheelchair racing pioneer and 'incredible man' Bob Hall dies aged 74
item.description: Bob Hall, who was the first wheelchair athlete to compete in a marathon before becoming a influential race chair designer, dies aged 74.

item.title: Australia's Jason Day rues missed opportunities after tough final day at the Masters
item.description: Australia's Jason Day stays positive after falling to 12th on a difficult final day at the Masters.

item.title: Two people missing after remote Queensland house fire
item.description: A crime scene has been established and two people in their 70s are unaccounted for following a house fire in a remote area of Queensland.

item.title: Cartel Paradise: A special investigation into the Pacific's drug superhighway
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Nigerian military air strike on market reportedly kills more than 100 civilians
item.description: Amnesty International calls for an independent investigation into an air strike on a Nigerian market, where the country's military says it was targeting militants.

item.title: Wreck Bay Aboriginal council to take on sole management of Booderee National Park in Jervis Bay
item.description: The Wreck Bay Aboriginal Community Council plans to take on sole management of Booderee National Park In May 2028.

item.title: The Strait of Hormuz blockade will cut off Iran's ports. Here's what that means
item.description: US president Donald Trump has ordered his nation's military to cut off all Iranian ports from maritime traffic passing through the Strait of Hormuz, one of the world's most vital shipping routes.

item.title: Anti-war protester to face court over 'From the River to the Sea' banner
item.description: Jim Dowling was arrested outside Boeing's headquarters in Brisbane earlier this year.

item.title: Donald Trump calls Pope Leo XIV 'weak' after pontiff calls for peace
item.description: Writing on social media after the pope called for peace, US President Donald Trump says the first American pope is "terrible for foreign policy" and he should "stop catering to the Radical Left".

item.title: She Matters memorial to women allegedly killed by men removed from Hosier Lane
item.description: The founder of Australian Femicide Watch says she is heartbroken over the removal of the She Matters mural in Hosier Lane dedicated to more than 200 women who have died since January 2025.

item.title: Prime minister names head of navy Mark Hammond as new defence chief
item.description: Vice Admiral Mark Hammond, who has spent 40 years in the navy, will replace current defence force chief David Johnston when he retires in July.

item.title: Blayze James Noble faces Alice Springs court charged with murdering girlfriend
item.description: NT Police have submitted more than 120 pieces of evidence in the case of a Queensland man who is charged with murdering his girlfriend on an outback road trip last week.

item.title: Record crowd for AFL Gather Round in South Australia as premier warns of 'lite' interstate version
item.description: South Australia's premier says he is "trying to hold the line" as Gather Round negotiations continue, with the 2026 event attracting record crowds.

item.title: Izzy Borlase leads host of Opals stars to sign new deals during WNBA free agency period
item.description: Izzy Borlase is rewarded for her MVP-winning season with the Bendigo Spirit as she pens a new deal with the Atlanta Dream in the WNBA.

item.title: Booking.com warns customers of possible data and security breach by 'unauthorised parties'
item.description: Some Booking.com customers are being warned their personal information may have been accessed by unauthorised third parties, prompting fresh concerns about the security of travellers' data.

item.title: Five of our favourite soup recipes for cool nights
item.description: Looking for new soup recipes as the weather cools? From minestrone to hot and sour dumplings, we have you covered.

item.title: Donald Trump's Strait of Hormuz blockade could further undermine global economic confidence
item.description: Donald Trump's planned United States naval blockade of Iranian vessels in the Strait of Hormuz reveals the president once again playing loose with the fortunes of financial markets and the global economy as he struggles to find a way out of the war that he and Israel started.

item.title: At this Canberra farmers' market, producers are making tough choices amid rising fuel prices
item.description: As fuel prices remain high amid the war in the Middle East, growers and fishers are among those having to make tough decisions to remain viable.

item.title: Teenage girl who 'weaponised' stolen cars denied bail over risk to community
item.description: A 13-year-old girl is facing court over 109 charges relating to incidents including allegedly driving into a cyclist.

item.title: Illawarra Hawks unimpressed as Basketball Australia probe clears NBL owner Larry Kestelman
item.description: Illawarra Hawks owner Jared Novelly's feud with NBL boss Larry Kestelman shows no signs of ending despite a BA investigation having concluded.

item.title: National medal for backyard astronomer Chris Stockdale who helped discover 100 exoplanets
item.description: From a backyard observatory in regional Victoria, Gippsland astronomer Chris Stockdale spends his nights helping confirm distant planets by tracking tiny changes in starlight that reveal new worlds beyond our solar system.

item.title: One Nation's Cory Bernardi approaches independent SA MPs in bid to form state opposition
item.description: One Nation SA leader Cory Bernardi has approached independent MPs to make the case for his party — instead of the Liberals — to be recognised as the state's official opposition.

item.title: Tasmanian student vaccinated without consent prompts council call for state government to run program
item.description: A Tasmanian mayor is calling on the state government to take on responsibility for running school immunisation programs, after a student was vaccinated without caregiver consent.

item.title: Zak Butters referred to AFL Tribunal after allegedly asking umpire 'how much are they paying you?'
item.description: Port Adelaide star Zak Butters will front the AFL Tribunal over an exchange with umpire Nick Foot.

item.title: Sailor records tense exchange between Iran and US in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: A tense exchange between the United States and Iran, recorded by a sailor in the Persian Gulf who has been speaking with the ABC, shows the extremely fraught situation in the Strait of Hormuz, as a fragile ceasefire hangs in the balance.

item.title: Federal MP Luke Gosling warns colleagues against Ben Roberts-Smith commentary
item.description: A federal MP and former commando has urged colleagues from across the aisle to refrain from speaking out about the arrest of accused war criminal Ben Roberts-Smith.

item.title: Horizontal Falls tourism shifts toward cultural experience
item.description: Tourism operators, the state government and traditional owners strike an agreement about the future of tourism at the famed Horizontal Falls in WA's Kimberley.
